item.title: Canadian banks, pension funds have provided billions in financing to ICE contractors, advocacy group says
item.description: Palantir, General Dynamics and L3Harris among companies that have received investment totalling about $35-billion

item.description: Fertiliser is another casualty of the closed Strait of Hormuz

item.description: The rich are increasingly relying on financial advisers to get them out of harm’s way during the Iran war

item.title: Who Struck It Rich in the Markets When Trump Postponed Bombing Iran?
item.description: A series of uncannily timed bets on the price of oil and stocks deserves a proper investigation. It’s far from clear that they’ll get one.

item.description: It’s the first Canadian bank to partner with the Montreal tech company

item.description: Canada must take the lead in forging a new global energy security pact

item.description: French company’s debut debt financing follows rising demand for alternatives to US groups

item.description: As investors seek to retrieve their money, the $22tn industry rejects comparisons with 2008. Regulators aren’t so sure

item.title: TotalEnergies made bumper profit on Middle East oil bet
item.description: Fewer contracts increased volatility and made it possible for a single trader to dominate the market

item.description: Disruption comes as investors see Jim Ratcliffe’s chemicals group boosted by energy market ructions from Iran conflict

item.title: UK ministers explore ‘targeted’ energy bill relief for those most in need
item.description: Lower-cost scheme could be delivered through councils under one option being considered

item.description: US pharmaceutical group ‘optimistic’ about reaching deal with British government

item.description: Spectre of another surge in inflation leaves struggling consumer goods groups with difficult decisions

item.description: Portfolio management should be based more on themes such as innovation

item.description: Latest data indicates victims reported more than $704-million in losses in 2025

item.description: Sunny lives with chronic fatigue and as a result, has to turn down work and spend more on things like Uber

item.description: Well-timed bets ahead of U.S. President’s policy surprises have potentially led to millions in profits for unknown traders

item.description: Extension is vital to guarantee predictability, business leaders say

item.description: Government bonds face one of worst months of past decade as investors warn of ‘deterioration’ in public finances

item.title: In a world of $100 oil, fast fashion loses its defensive charms
item.description: Polyester-heavy retailers could soon start to feel the cost of their exposure to higher oil prices

item.title: Korea weighs inheritance tax based on book value, not market prices

item.description: Rebels’ entry to month-long conflict could further hurt global shipping if they again target vessels in Red Sea

item.description: Leonard will remain an adviser, with a focus on supporting a new investment strategy, company says

item.description: The debate over North Sea oil and gas resembles a bar brawl between opponents who can’t see straight

item.title: The rippling effect: Your work reaches further than you think
item.description: New research finds 86 per cent of Canadian workers, and almost the same amount of American workers, agree with the statement ‘A lot of people can be affected by how well I do my work’
